agent_outputs.raw: The fold-local augmentation design prevents contamination. Splitting at the original-image level before augmentation means no augmented image can appear in validation or test. Part-level physical-unit clustering prevents near-duplicate original crossings. Later-week raw test provides temporal separation. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

Heavy augmentation inside training folds is standard practice for imbalanced detection tasks. The key is that validation and test receive only raw images. The later-week holdout provides strong external validity for this manufacturing process and camera. The claim is appropriately scoped. No methodological flaw. VERDICT: defense_wins

The nested cross-validation design with fold-local augmentation is correctly implemented as described. The later-week raw test set is a strong final evaluation. Part-level clustering at physical-unit granularity prevents same-unit contamination. Scope is disclosed. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

synthesis: All three assessors find the evaluation valid. The fold-local augmentation, split-before-augment, physical-unit clustering, and later-week raw test set form a coherent defense against contamination concerns. ETD constraint: unanimous defense wins.
all_issues_raised: augmentation contamination concern — eliminated by fold-local design and split order

empirical_test.condition: Audit pipeline to confirm split-before-augment; verify fold-local augmentation in training pass only; verify raw later-week test; verify physical-unit-level part grouping with no boundary crossings
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Any augmented or near-duplicate image from a training unit appears in validation or test
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: All pipeline checks confirm correct order and isolation; test is raw later-week data; no unit-level crossings
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Core pipeline is correct but part grouping incomplete for some edge-case batches
